title: Amblyanthera pavonii
taxonomy: Amblyanthera pavonii is classified as a species of plant in the family Passifloraceae. This family consists of approximately 950 species of flowering plants, commonly known as passionflowers. Within the Passifloraceae family, Amblyanthera pavonii is further classified as a member of the tribe Passifloreae. The genus Amblyanthera is relatively small, containing only a few species of plants native to the Americas. The classification of Amblyanthera pavonii is an example of the ongoing process of taxonomic refinement in the field of botany.
uses: Amblyanthera pavonii has been traditionally used in folk medicine in various parts of the world, particularly in Latin America. Its root is believed to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs.
